lethargy: Puppies with low energy may have low blood sugar or infection. Ensure they are hydrated.
appetite: Loss of appetite for more than 24 hours requires a veterinary consultation.
itching: Frequent scratching often indicates fleas, mites, or seasonal allergies.
vomiting: Occasional vomiting may be dietary. Persistent vomiting with lethargy or loss of appetite requires immediate veterinary evaluation.
diarrhea: Mild diarrhea may resolve with bland diet. If persistent for more than 2 days or accompanied by blood, seek veterinary care.
coughing: Occasional coughing may be allergies or irritation. Persistent cough, especially with labored breathing, requires veterinary attention.
limping: Sudden limping may indicate injury or pain. Check for swelling or heat in the limb. Lameness lasting more than a few hours needs assessment.
ear_infection: Signs include excessive head shaking, ear odor, or discharge. Ear infections can worsen quickly and require veterinary treatment.
skin_infection: Redness, rashes, or oozing sores may indicate infection. Keep area clean and dry. Veterinary evaluation recommended to prevent spread.
weight_loss: Unintended weight loss combined with appetite changes warrants a veterinary check to rule out underlying conditions.
excessive_thirst: Significantly increased water intake may indicate diabetes, kidney issues, or infection. Schedule a veterinary visit for evaluation.
behavioral_change: Sudden changes in behavior such as aggression, anxiety, or withdrawal can signal pain or illness. Observe for other symptoms and contact your vet.
eye_discharge: Clear or watery discharge may be minor. Thick, colored discharge or redness requires prompt veterinary examination.
sneezing: Occasional sneezing is usually normal. Frequent sneezing with nasal discharge may indicate infection or allergies.
panting: Excessive panting in cool conditions or at rest may indicate pain, anxiety, fever, or heart issues. Have your pet checked by a veterinarian.
